Container.Name: snorcal
Container.Overview:
Self-hosted 3D slicing hub for OrcaSlicer, BambuStudio, and Snapmaker Orca. Web UI for uploading models, configuring slice settings, painting face colors, and shipping gcode to Moonraker/Bambu/Snapmaker printers.
Requires two companion containers: 1. snorcal-slicer (ghcr.io/davidphillips2/snorcal-slicer) — Ubuntu + Xvfb + slicer binaries 2. redis (use the redis:7-alpine template from Community Apps)
All three containers must share the same /data volume so the app can read gcode produced by the slicer.
Container.Requires: snorcal-slicer container + redis container. Create a custom Docker network (e.g. `snorcal`) and attach all three so they can resolve each other by name.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the Snorcal web UI.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the SQLite DB, uploaded models, and generated gcode. MUST be the same host path mounted on the snorcal-slicer container.

Config.Name: REDIS_HOST
Config.Description: Hostname of the Redis container. Must be reachable on the same Docker network.

Config.Name: SLICER_URL
Config.Description: Internal URL of the snorcal-slicer container. The slicer container must be named `snorcal-slicer` on the shared Docker network.

Config.Name: NODE_ENV
Config.Description: Set to production so backend serves the bundled frontend.

Config.Name: DEBUG_PRINTER
Config.Description: Set to 1 to enable verbose MQTT/printer adapter logs.
